titleStmt.title: Fatima Harran
SHAWI Corpus
titleStmt.funder: Funded by the FWF Austrian Science Fund under the number P 33574

publicationStmt.distributor: Austrian Center for Digital Humanities and Cultural Heritage

prefixDef.p: Private URIs using the teiCorpusHeader prefix are pointers to any element in the SHAWI teiCorpus document.
Private URIs using the share prefix are pointers to audio file residing on the SHAWI network share.
Private URIs using the geo prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a place element in VICAV Geodata list.
Private URIs using the zotid prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a biblStruct element in the TEI export of the VICAV Zotero Group library.
Private URIs using the dict prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a entry in the SHAWI glossary.
Private URIs using the rend prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a rendition element in VICAV rendition library.
Private URIs using the assets prefix are pointers to the location of the SHAWI media assets.
Private URIs using the vtc prefix are pointers to the list of VICAV text classes.

spanGrp.span: One day, her holiness Fatimah asked her father saying, “O my father, who will be my neighbour in paradise? Who will be my neighbour in paradise?”
He said to her, “The daughter of so-and-so, the wife of so-and-so,” He named that certain person, that certain man. His wife will be your neighbour in Paradise.

Anyways, she became curious and said, “I want to meet her. What did she do to deserve this? I am the Fatimah of the prophet, the wife of his holiness Ali.” [to his elder daughter: Take her outside, my daughter!]
How has she deserved this? I mean, she will be my neighbour in paradise. How did she attain this?
She had become curious and said, “I want to go and see her.” So, she went to her and knocked on the door. She had asked about the so-and-so and where his house was.

She knocked on the door, let’s say she knocked on the door of her house. The woman came to the door and asked, “Who are you, sister?” She replied, “In fact, I am Fatimah, the (daughter) of the prophet, and I came to see you, to visit you.”
She said, “By God, I cannot let you in.” “Why?” she said, “You won’t accept me?” She replied, “My husband…” let’s say (he was called) Ali or Mahmud; whatever his name was, I don’t know.
“He went out to graze the sheep on the pasture; he is with the sheep. So, only when he returns from the pasture and I get permission from him, only then will I be able to welcome you inside.
If I do not get permission from my husband, I will not let you inside, and I cannot welcome you.”
She said, “Alright. When should I come again?” She replied, “Come tomorrow!” She said, “Alright."

But she noticed that the woman, the shepherd’s wife, was not sitting inside the house. Instead, she had placed water and bread under the sun, outside in the heat. She had placed the water in the pot in the heat.
She had placed the bread outside; she had had put the bread outside. Of course, she didn’t ask her and didn’t go inside. Fatimah, the daughter of the prophet, went back to her house.
The next day she went there again having placed Hasan in her lap. Hasan and Husayn were young; they were still toddlers, her twins.
She had taken Hasan and said, “Today, I want to take Hasan with me.” She left Husayn at home.

She carried Hasan in her lap and approached her again, knocked on the door the other day and (was expected to) enter. She said, “By God, I came to you again. Didn’t you say, ‘Come tomorrow!’ Have you got permission from your husband to let me in?”
“Who is this?” she asked. “The one in your lap?” – “He is my son Hasan,” she replied.

She said, “Sister, I have permission only for you alone. I have no permission for the one in your lap. I haven’t got permission for him. You didn’t bring him yesterday, so I cannot take the one in your lap, the child.”
She said, “What should I do?” The woman replied, “Go away today as well and come back tomorrow!” She said, “When you came yesterday you didn’t bring the one in your lap.”
“Take him and go home, then come agaub tomorrow! I will get permission for your son, the one in your lap, Hasan. Come to me tomorrow!”
She said, “Alright!” Fatimah, the daughter of the prophet, went there again; she was patient. She went the next day again there; it was the third day.
his time, Fatimah placed both Hasan and Husayn in her lap and brought them with her.
She knocked on the door again. The woman looked and said, “O sister!”
“What?” she said. “I came to you again as we agreed.” The woman replied, “Yes, but yesterday you brought one in your lap and today they have become two.”
“I have permission from my husband for one only. But today you have brought two. So, once again, I cannot take you inside.”

“Go until my husband returns in the evening and I will tell him: ‘Fatimah, the daughter of the prophet, she has Hasan and Husayn in her lap.’ So, I can get permission for them to let you in tomorrow.”
Fatimah, the daughter of the prophet, went away. Her husband returned at sunset, and she said to him, “Today, she came with two (children) in her lap as well.” He said, “Oh, you crazy one!”
“Fatimah, the daughter of the prophet, comes to your door, and one day you say there is one in her lap and such! Let her in!”
“Even if the whole city is with her, let her in!” Do not turn away Fatimah, the daughter of the prophet, anymore!”
She said, “Alright!” The next day she came again and that time she only had one in her lap. She said to her, “My sister, please come in, come in, come in!”
I have permission for everyone from my husband, and he even got angry with me.” She took her inside.
Fatimah, the daughter of the prophet, looked and (expected) the woman sitting inside. (But) she was in the courtyard of the house.
She was walking around saying, “Housh, koosh, housh, chish, ho, brr!” She was pretending to graze the sheep in the courtyard of the house, although there were no sheep there.
She said, “My sister, what are you doing? There are no sheep! There is no pasture here. What are you doing, saying ‘Housh and koosh and qof and...?”
She said, “My sister, my husband is grazing the sheep; he is now in the mountains, under the sun.”
“And he must drink hot water and eat dry bread and always has to say, ‘Hesh and koosh!’”
“I am here. Would it be fair for me to sit in the shade, rest in my seat like this, and pamper myself?”
“He is grazing the sheep over there, and I am grazing them here in the courtyard of the house. I do the same so that it will get good (reward) for me.”
She said, “Look at my water! This water. He drinks hot water, and I too drink hot water.”
“He drinks water with... he has only dry bread to eat. And so, I eat dry bread too. I do not eat soft bread.”
“My husband is outside on the pasture, and everything he does, I do it here as well. I should not be sitting comfortably in the shade.”

“Ha!” Fatimah, the daughter of the prophet, said, “This means that my father was right. For this reason, my father said to me, “She will be your neighbour in paradise.” As a reward for her actions. God bless you!
